subject: Re: disk1.cab error with Warp_1.0.9 install
body:
Hi Jacob,

Some tips here, did you come across this? https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/forum/all/why-is-disk1cab-corrupt-on-every-software-download/83ec4491-52b7-4252-9619-0e487fd6d5e9

Cheers,

Alister

Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity

> On 2 Aug 2022, at 20:49, Jacob Anderson <jacronand13@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> ﻿Fresh Windows 10 install today with the goal of using the workstation for Warp/M. After  assuring the pre-requisites were in order (.Net Framework 4.8 downloaded, Visual C++ 2017 Redistributable installed + restarted, + newest NVIDIA drivers for a 3090 RTX), I downloaded Warp_1.0.9. I then ran the installer. 
> 
> I get the error "The cabinent file 'disk1.cab' required for this installation is corrupt and cannot be used. This could indicate a network error, an error reading from the CD-ROM, or a problem with this package."
> 
> On clicking "Retry" the error recurs. On clicking ignore I then get a new error: "FDI server error".
> 
> I performed an iteration of deleting the installer and redownloading and running the installer. This reproduced the issue. 
> 
> I am curious if anyone has encountered and overcome this problem on the install. Or if this installer error is diagnostic of another problem.
> 
> 
> 
> 
> --
